---
title: "Benchmarking Fixed Effects Implementations and Solvers"
description: "Runtime comparison of PyFixest, fixest, and FixedEffectModels.jl on real-world HDFE datasets."
categories: [performance, fixed effects, benchmarks]
jupyter: python3
format:
  html:
    html-table-processing: none
toc: true
toc-title: "On this page"
toc-location: left
---

## Overview

[Sergio Correia's HDFE benchmark suite](https://www.scorreia.com/data/hdfe/index.html)
consists of a collection of real-world datasets that stress different graph topologies and are the most extensive collection of public benchmark data sets for HDFE solvers we are aware of.

We benchmark PyFixest against the following references:

- [**fixest**](https://lrberge.github.io/fixest/), which is the reference HDFE implementation in R. `fixest` implements a MAP-style solver with Irons-Tuck acceleration and other optimizations.
- [**FixedEffectModels.jl**](https://github.com/FixedEffects/FixedEffectModels.jl), which is the reference Julia implementation and implements an LSMR solver with diagonal preconditioning.

All benchmarks are run on Alex' local machine (Mac mini, Apple M4, 10 cores, 16 GB RAM, macOS 15.3.1).

We fit two-way fixed effects regressions using each package's defaults: multicollinear variables are dropped (using package-specific algorithms), singletons are optionally dropped, and iid inference is used. We run each benchmark with and without singleton dropping. We use a tolerance of `1e-8`. Note that convergence criteria differ across implementations, so the tolerance is only a common stopping threshold and does not guarantee identical convergence behavior.

::: {.callout-note}
Benchmarks for [**reghdfe**](https://scorreia.com/software/reghdfe/) (Stata) can be found on [Sergio Correia's website](https://www.scorreia.com/data/hdfe/index.html). Note, however, that those numbers were collected roughly nine years ago on older hardware, so Python, R, and Julia implementations have a substantial hardware advantage in any direct comparison.
:::

::: {.callout}
For an explanation why different fixed effects / graph structures lead to differences in solver performance, please take a look at the [When Are Fixed Effects Estimations Difficult?](difficult-fixed-effects.md) vignette.
:::


## Benchmarks

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | fig-width: 14
# | fig-height: 6
from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
from matplotlib.transforms import blended_transform_factory
import pandas as pd

RESULTS_DIR = Path("../../benchmarks/results")

# Colors / markers from benchmarks/modular/plotting.py
ALGO_META: dict[str, dict] = {
    "pyfixest-map":     {"label": "PyFixest MAP (baseline)", "color": "#047857", "marker": "o", "jitter": -0.22, "zorder": 4, "size": 90},
    "pyfixest-within":  {"label": "PyFixest CG",             "color": "#16A34A", "marker": "s", "jitter": -0.07, "zorder": 5, "size": 80},
    "fixest":           {"label": "fixest (R)",               "color": "#2563EB", "marker": "^", "jitter": +0.07, "zorder": 5, "size": 80},
    "FixedEffectModels":{"label": "FEM.jl (Julia)",           "color": "#EAB308", "marker": "D", "jitter": +0.22, "zorder": 5, "size": 80},
}

CORREIA_DATASETS = [
    "credit2", "credit", "soccer", "synthetic-complete",
    "synthetic-uniform-easy", "synthetic-uniform-hard",
    "synthetic-uniform-harder", "synthetic-assortative",
    "synthetic-zigzag", "enron", "github", "patents",
    "workers", "schools", "directors",
]
REAL_DATASETS = {d for d in CORREIA_DATASETS if not d.startswith("synthetic")}
SYNTH_CORREIA = {d for d in CORREIA_DATASETS if d.startswith("synthetic")}
FIXEST_DGPS   = {"fixest-simple", "fixest-difficult"}

CORREIA_ALGO_MAP = {
    "pyfixest-map": "pyfixest-map", "pyfixest-within": "pyfixest-within",
    "fixest": "fixest", "FixedEffectModels": "FixedEffectModels",
}
MAIN_BACKEND_MAP = {
    "pyfixest (rust-map)": "pyfixest-map", "pyfixest (rust-cg)": "pyfixest-within",
    "fixest-map": "fixest", "FEM.jl (lsmr)": "FixedEffectModels",
}

GAP = 1.5


def _fmt(t: float) -> str:
    return f"{t:.1f}s"


def _load_correia() -> pd.DataFrame:
    df = pd.read_csv(RESULTS_DIR / "correia-benchmarks.csv")
    df = df[df["dataset"].isin(CORREIA_DATASETS) & df["success"].astype(bool) & df["time"].notna()].copy()
    df["algo"] = df["algo"].map(CORREIA_ALGO_MAP)
    return df[["dataset", "algo", "singleton_drop", "time"]]


def _load_main() -> pd.DataFrame:
    frames = [pd.read_csv(p) for p in RESULTS_DIR.glob("feols_bench__*.csv")]
    if not frames:
        return pd.DataFrame(columns=["dataset", "algo", "singleton_drop", "time"])
    df = pd.concat(frames, ignore_index=True)
    df = df[
        (df["n_fe"] == 2) & (df["model_k"] == 1) & (df["n_obs"] == 10_000_000)
        & df["backend"].isin(MAIN_BACKEND_MAP) & df["success"].astype(bool) & df["time"].notna()
    ].copy()
    df["algo"] = df["backend"].map(MAIN_BACKEND_MAP)
    df["dataset"] = "fixest-" + df["dgp"]
    df["singleton_drop"] = True  # fixest DGPs drop singletons
    return df[["dataset", "algo", "singleton_drop", "time"]]


def _sorted_by_map(agg: pd.DataFrame, datasets: set[str], singleton: bool) -> list[str]:
    base = (
        agg[(agg["algo"] == "pyfixest-map") & (agg["singleton_drop"] == singleton) & agg["dataset"].isin(datasets)]
        .sort_values("time")
    )
    ordered = base["dataset"].tolist()
    return ordered + sorted(d for d in datasets if d not in ordered)


def _make_figure(agg: pd.DataFrame, singleton: bool) -> plt.Figure:
    real_order  = _sorted_by_map(agg, REAL_DATASETS, singleton)
    synth_order = _sorted_by_map(agg, SYNTH_CORREIA, singleton)
    if singleton:
        synth_order += _sorted_by_map(agg, FIXEST_DGPS, singleton=True)

    real_xs = {ds: float(i) for i, ds in enumerate(real_order)}
    s0 = len(real_order) + GAP
    synth_xs = {ds: s0 + i for i, ds in enumerate(synth_order)}
    ds_xs = {**real_xs, **synth_xs}
    all_ds = real_order + synth_order

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 6))

    sub_all  = agg[agg["singleton_drop"] == singleton].copy()
    baseline = (
        sub_all[sub_all["algo"] == "pyfixest-map"][["dataset", "time"]]
        .rename(columns={"time": "base_time"})
    )

    for algo, meta in ALGO_META.items():
        sub = (
            sub_all[sub_all["algo"] == algo]
            .merge(baseline, on="dataset", how="inner")
            .copy()
        )
        sub = sub[sub["dataset"].isin(all_ds)]
        if sub.empty:
            continue
        sub["ratio"] = sub["time"] / sub["base_time"]
        sub["x"] = sub["dataset"].map(ds_xs) + meta["jitter"]

        if algo == "pyfixest-map":
            sub["ratio"] = 1.0
            ax.scatter(sub["x"], sub["ratio"], color=meta["color"], marker=meta["marker"],
                       s=meta["size"], zorder=meta["zorder"], label=meta["label"],
                       edgecolors="white", linewidths=0.5)
            for _, row in sub.iterrows():
                ax.annotate(_fmt(row["base_time"]), xy=(row["x"], 1.0), xytext=(0, 6),
                            textcoords="offset points", ha="center", va="bottom",
                            fontsize=5.5, color=meta["color"])
        else:
            valid = sub[sub["ratio"].notna()]
            ax.scatter(valid["x"], valid["ratio"], color=meta["color"], marker=meta["marker"],
                       s=meta["size"], zorder=meta["zorder"], label=meta["label"],
                       edgecolors="white", linewidths=0.5)
            for _, row in valid.iterrows():
                actual = row["ratio"] * row["base_time"]
                above = row["ratio"] >= 1.0
                ax.annotate(_fmt(actual), xy=(row["x"], row["ratio"]),
                            xytext=(0, 6 if above else -8),
                            textcoords="offset points", ha="center",
                            va="bottom" if above else "top",
                            fontsize=5.5, color=meta["color"])

    sep_x = len(real_order) - 0.5 + GAP / 2
    ax.axvline(sep_x, color="#CBD5E1", lw=1.2, ls="--", zorder=0)
    trans = blended_transform_factory(ax.transData, ax.transAxes)
    real_mid  = (len(real_order) - 1) / 2
    synth_mid = s0 + (len(synth_order) - 1) / 2
    for x_mid, lbl in [(real_mid, "Real datasets"), (synth_mid, "Synthetic datasets")]:
        ax.text(x_mid, 1.01, lbl, ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=10,
                color="#475569", fontstyle="italic", transform=trans, clip_on=False)

    ax.axhline(1.0, color="#94A3B8", lw=0.8, ls="--", zorder=1)
    ax.set_yscale("log")
    ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda y, _: f"{y:g}×"))

    def _xlabel(ds: str) -> str:
        if ds.startswith("fixest-"):
            return ds.replace("fixest-", "") + "\n(fixest DGP)"
        return ds.replace("synthetic-", "synth-")

    ax.set_xticks([ds_xs[ds] for ds in all_ds])
    ax.set_xticklabels([_xlabel(ds) for ds in all_ds], rotation=45, ha="right", fontsize=8)
    ax.set_ylabel("Runtime ratio vs PyFixest MAP (log scale)", fontsize=10)
    ax.grid(True, which="both", axis="y", alpha=0.25, ls=":", color="#64748B")
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)
    ax.spines[["top", "right"]].set_visible(False)

    suffix = "with singleton dropping" if singleton else "without singleton dropping"
    ax.set_title(
        f"Runtime relative to PyFixest MAP ({suffix}, Correia 2-way FE, tol=1e-8)\n"
        "Datasets sorted by increasing PyFixest MAP runtime within each group",
        fontsize=10, pad=20,
    )
    handles, labels = ax.get_legend_handles_labels()
    fig.legend(handles, labels, loc="lower center", bbox_to_anchor=(0.5, -0.01),
               ncol=4, fontsize=9, framealpha=0.95, edgecolor="#CBD5E1",
               handletextpad=0.5, columnspacing=1.2)
    plt.tight_layout(rect=[0, 0.07, 1, 1])
    return fig


raw = pd.concat([_load_correia(), _load_main()], ignore_index=True)
agg = raw.groupby(["dataset", "algo", "singleton_drop"], observed=True)["time"].median().reset_index()

fig1 = _make_figure(agg, singleton=True)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | fig-width: 14
# | fig-height: 6
fig2 = _make_figure(agg, singleton=False)
plt.show()
```

## Reproducing these numbers

To run the benchmarks yourself, you can use the `pixi` tasks below, which will automatically install all required (open source) dependencies, create synethetic data sets and run the benchmarks. You will have to download the Correia datasets from [Correia's website](https://www.scorreia.com/data/hdfe/index.html) and place them in the `benchmarks/modular/data/correia` directory.

```bash
# benchmark fixest-dgp's
pixi run benchmark
# benchmark R, Julia, Pyton on the Correia datasets
pixi run benchmark-correia
```
